law: Before you run the sample code project in Xcode, make sure: Your iOS device is running either iOS 12.5 or 13.5 or later.
To get permission to use this entitlement, see .
Users must explicitly authorize an app to use Exposure Notifications.
The class manages the user’s private data, defined as a series of properties that all use this property wrapper.
Each time the app calls this method, the user must authorize the transaction.
This authorization lasts for up to five days and should be requested only if the app determines that the user is about to take a test and the app has a way to determine the results.
PHAs must notify users that they’ve tested positive before the app calls .
They must also notify users that their keys are being submitted to the PHA’s key server.
To prevent runtime errors, Exposure Notifications apps for iOS 13.5 and iOS 13.6 must fall back to this version.
Version 2 — When available, apps should use this version of the API.
